Repetitions

You are given a DNA sequence consisting of characters A, C, G, and T.
Find the length of the longest repetition in the sequence.
A repetition is a substring consisting of identical characters.

Input:
The first line contains a string s (1 ≤ |s| ≤ 10^6) consisting of characters A, C, G, and T.

Output:
Print one integer — the length of the longest repetition.

Sample Input:
ATTCGGGA

Sample Output:
3
